person.name: Lakshman Shenoy
person.bio: I write deeply researched articles on technology, science, finance, and society, and build open source tools along the way.

beliefs.title: Complexity deserves honesty, not simplification
beliefs.description: Accessibility should not come from flattening the truth. Depth changes understanding.

beliefs.title: Writing is thinking made legible
beliefs.description: Clear writing is evidence of clear thought. If it is muddy on the page, it is muddy in mind.

beliefs.title: Open source is a form of generosity
beliefs.description: Useful solutions should be shareable. Good tools compound when they are open.

beliefs.title: Depth over frequency
beliefs.description: Publishing cadence is secondary to quality. Fewer pieces, stronger signal.

beliefs.title: First principles over received wisdom
beliefs.description: Assumptions should be rebuilt from fundamentals, not inherited from consensus.

lab.title: About ShenoyLabs
paragraphs: ShenoyLabs started as a place to put things that did not have a home elsewhere: research notes, project postmortems, and half-finished ideas that became complete ones.
A lab is where things are tested, revised, and occasionally discarded. Some ideas fail. Some pieces are rewritten as understanding evolves. That is part of the process.
What stays constant: no ads, no sponsor pressure, no algorithmic publishing. Work gets published when it is worth reading.
lab.quote: Think. Learn. Solve. in that order, and never in a hurry.

fallbacks.readingDetail: Tracking incoming ideas from recent notes and drafts.
fallbacks.buildingDetail: Improving the site and open-source tooling stack.
fallbacks.thinkingDetail: Exploring practical ways to make better decisions under volatility.
